_ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-change">Archive Tab, Collections Overview, and Bug Fixes</div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-description">A new Archive tab has been added, allowing players to browse all in-game collections and easily view the bonuses associated with each set. This new section provides a clearer overview of progression and completion status. Various bugs have also been fixed to improve overall stability and user experience across the site.</div></div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-item"><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-date">2026-01-18</div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-change">Agenda Integration, Item Enchant Guide, and Homepage Redesign</div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-description">Game agenda data has been integrated, allowing players to easily view upcoming events and activities. A complete list of item enchantments has been added along with a brand-new dedicated guide (Item Enchants), including direct links between enchantments and their associated items. Items can now be filtered by attributes, and evolutive item stats are properly referenced. The homepage has been fully redesigned, offering quick shortcuts to key sections of the site. Additionally, several bugs have been fixed, including an issue where certain crafting recipes were no longer displayed.</div></div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-item"><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-date">2026-01-07</div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-change">Sprite Tab Added and Interface Improvements</div><div _ngcontent-ng-c2298885063 class="update-description">A new Sprite tab has been added, gathering all sprites available in the game. Instructions","8579":"Crystal Purchase","8580":"CS","8581":"Game Master (GM) Support","8582":"Technical Support","8583":"Ever since the dawn of time, the world has been held in balance by the power of the sprites. It is said that in§the earliest days, before the people of Saphael had split into competing tribes and factions, humans could interact§with these mystical creatures. In those times, humans lived in a state of harmony with Nature,§and sprites were plentiful throughout the land.§§Unfortunately, this age of tranquility would not last. As the tribes of Saphael continued to splinter and sever ties,§their natural bond with the sprites also began to fade. They eventually spread to all corners of the world a§nd built settlements -- razing forests, clearing fields,§and tearing down mountains to erect their majestic cities.§§Driven by an insatiable desire for power over their human brethren, some of these factions ventured deep into§the earth where they unlocked dark and ancient secrets that would ultimately threaten to destroy all of mankind.§Perhaps chief among these dangers were the great demon lords and dragons that terrorized§the people of Saphael and set their villages and cities ablaze.§§All would have been lost if it were not for the emergence of the Sprite Messengers. These rare and remarkable§people had managed to preserve their link with the sprites while the rest of humanity had moved on.§The sprites granted these individuals magical powers to combat the evil and help restore balance to the world.§The whole of Saphael would unite behind these legendary few and together, they would beat back§the forces of darkness that had once threatened to overwhelm them.§§The peace was short-lived. The various factions that had united under the leadership of the Sprite Messengers§soon returned to their ways and went to war with each other. Worse, even the Messengers came to be affected.§Although they had previously seemed above such petty divisions, many of them took sides in the conflict§while only a handful remained to try and ease hostilities. The period that ensued§would come to be known as the \"Great Saphael War.\"§§The war lasted","8584":"Sprites are mystical creatures that are rumored to have existed since the beginning of time.§They remain unseen to most people, choosing to reveal themselves only to a select few.§They are inextricably linked to Nature and it is their power that holds the world in balance.§When this balance is threatened, Sprites seek out and grant their powers to Sprite Messengers.","8585":"Each player is the descendent of Sprite Messengers, born to choose a guardian Sprite to accompany them§through their lives. Sprite Messengers of Siwa Island must face the trials when they come of age,§and if they pass the trials, they will begin their journey of rescuing Sprites on the Saphael mainland.§§For a Sprite Messenger, one should not only train oneself in games, but also cultivates Sprites as the most§important mission, let sprites return to the nature once they grow up, and resuce Saphael from being devastation.§§In addition to their own Guadian Sprites, the players will encounter more sprites in their adventure.§It's your task to cultivate these naughty and adorable sprites and take them back to nature...","8586":"◎Movement:§In the game, you can control the character using both the keyboard§and mouse simultaneously.§§＜Keyboard Controls＞§W: Move forward§S: Move backward§A: Turn left§D: Turn right§Q: Move left§E: Move right§When moving with the keyboard, hold down the left mouse button§and drag to quickly turn left or right.§§＜Mouse Controls＞§Left mouse button: Click on the ground at the destination to move.§§◎Leap:§SPACE: Character leaps§§◎Auto-Walk:§Num Lock: Character walks forward automatically§§◎Sit / Stand:§X: Press once to sit, press again to stand§§◎Weapons Display Toggle:§Z: Switch between melee and ranged weapons display§§◎Variable Form Equipment Display Toggle:§U: Switch between Form 1 and Form 2 display§§◎Camera Control:§Hold down the right mouse button and drag: Rotate the camera","8587":"@Normal Attack§Right-click on the enemy target with the mouse: it will automatically switch based on distance§Close range / Ranged Weapons to perform the attack§§@Skill Attack: Select a target before performing a skill attack§1. Right-click on the skill icon to cast the skill§2. Use the corresponding hotkey to cast the skill","8588":"◎Normal:§Type \"/s message\" in the chat box and press Enter,§or press CTRL+S to switch the speaking channel to the Normal Channel.§§◎Whisper:§Type \"/w message\" in the chat box and press Enter,§to switch the speaking channel to the Whisper Channel.§§◎Region:§Type \"/y message\" in the chat box and press Enter,§or press CTRL+Y to switch the speaking channel to the Region Channel.§§◎Party:§Type \"/p message\" in the chat box and press Enter,§or press CTRL+P to switch the speaking channel to the Party Channel.§§◎Guild:§Type \"/g message\" in the chat box and press Enter,§or press CTRL+G to switch the speaking channel to the Guild Channel.§§◎Whisper:§In any channel, type \"/t player name message\"§and press Enter to send a message to that player, without changing§the current speaking channel.§§◎Whisper Response:§In any channel, type \"/r message\" and press Enter,§or press CTRL+R to reply to the last whisper, without changing§the current speaking channel.","8589":"*Menu Hotkeys§§Character Menu:C: Open Character stats/equipment window. Backpack:§§B: Open Item Inventory§Skill:§§K: Open Skill / Talent / Mastery / Emote Menu§§§Adventure LogsL: Open the Quest Log / Records§Social Link:§§O: Open the Social Interface§System:§§Esc: Open System Menu options§§Map:§M: Open full map","8590":"@Customization Method:§The 12-grid shortcut bar at the bottom left of the game screen allows you to freely drag and place§icons for items, equipment, and skills, making it convenient for quick use or casting.§A total of 10 pages of shortcut bars are available for customization.§§1. Items: Right-click on an item to use it.§2. Equipment: Right-click on equipment to equip or unequip it.§3. Skills: Right-click on a skill to cast it.§§@Corresponding Hotkeys:§In addition to right-clicking, you can also use the corresponding hotkeys to cast.§Hotkeys for the bottom shortcut bar: The number row on the keyboard from 1 to =§Shortcut bar page switching (10 pages in total): Shift+1 to 0§Hotkeys for the top shortcut bar: CTRL+1 to =; ALT+1 to =","8591":"*Hide Interface:Alt + H: Disables Heads-Up Display (HUD) and User Interface (UI).§§Useful for taking attractive screenshots.§Select Party Member:F1-F5: Cycle through the characters currently in your party§§§Open Sprite Cottage:§§F6-F8: Open different Sprite interface panels§Auto-Targeting:Tab: Automatically targets the closest monster§§§Support:§§T: Targets the monster of the selected player§Auto-Follow:F: Automatically follows the targeted player§§§Take Screenshot:PrintScreen: Saves a screenshot§§§Close Windows:§Esc: Close all open in-game windows","8592":"@ Job Transition Explanation:§At the beginning, players start as Novices. When they reach level 5, they must choose a trainee§profession and complete a transition quest before they can continue leveling up.§§At level 15, players officially end their trainee profession career. After completing the second transition§quest, players not only can continue leveling up, but also learn more specialized job skills.§§After reaching level 30, players will face a crucial divergence in their career. During the third transition§quest, players need to choose their preferred profession from two options in order to learn more advanced job skills.§§@ Warrior Introduction:§A profession skilled in close combat, proficient in using various weapons. They focus on strengthening§DEF to withstand more DMG and protect their teammates, thus they can wear heavy metal armor and shields.§§In the future, they can advance to become a Berserker, specializing in DMG output, or a Paladin, focusing§on Guardianship abilities. Regardless of the chosen direction, Warrior professions always take the vanguard role in the team.§§@ Archer Introduction:§A profession skilled in long-range attack, proficient in using bows, arrows, and other long-range weapons.§Even in close combat, they can make good use of the advantages of dual-wielding, launching dazzling§attack to accumulate physical damage. They prioritize agility in their actions and§are accustomed to wearing comfortable leather equipment.§§In the future, they can choose to become a Ranger, emphasizing long-range combat to keep enemies at a distance,§or an Assassin, capable of silently killing in melee. Regardless of the chosen direction,§Archer professions play the roles of offense and sniper in the team.§§@ Priest Introduction:§A profession skilled in healing and providing support. Besides using staffs, they can also use hammers.§They are versatile in both magic and physical abilities. Although they mainly wear fabric-made equipment§for ease of casting, their ability to wield shields increases their survival chances.§§In the future, they can choose to become a Cleric, possessing powerful healing skills, or a Sage,§who summons or transforms into creatures from the Nature realm as their main attack force. Regardless§of the chosen direction, Priest professions have powerful support skills§and are indispensable for prolonging the team's combat effectiveness.§§@ Mage Introduction:§A profession skilled in magic combat and elemental control. They can only use staffs and wear lightweight robes for casting convenience. However, the damage power produced through magic is unmatched by other professions.§§In the future, they can also transition to become an Elementalist, specializing in elemental control, or a Necromancer, capable of summoning dark entities. Regardless of the chosen direction, Mage professions provide powerful magic damage output in the team, making them essential for increasing monster-killing speed.","8593":"@Skills§Depending on the player's character class, different class skills (active skills) can be learned as the level increases.§Properly utilizing these skills can bring greater benefits to battles.§§On the right side of the player's avatar, in addition to displaying health and energy, there are 5 empty spaces§for gathering points. Gathering points can be increased through gathering skills,§allowing for the use of more powerful finishing moves.§1. Gathering Skills: Skills that increase gathering points when used.§2. Finishing Moves: Skills that require additional consumption of gathering points. At least 1 gathering point§is needed to use them, and the more gathering points there are (up to a maximum of 5),§the stronger the finishing move's power.§§@Talents§Talents represent the character's passive skills. Players will obtain different patterns in the game to synthesize§various talents. Once a talent item is used, the corresponding talent will be permanently learned.§Players can view all the talents they have learned on the talent page.§§Players can freely equip the talents they have learned into the talent slots on the left side, activatin§g the passive effects granted by those talents.§§As the level increases, the number of talent slots that can be equipped on the character will gradually§increase. The opening order and restrictions for talents are as follows:§1. Character 1st Transformation (LV6): Earth Talents slot is opened.§2. Character 2nd Transformation (LV16): Star Talents slot is opened.§3. Character 3rd Transformation (LV31): Moon Talents slot is opened.§4. At LV46, Sun Talents slot is opened through a quest.§5. At LV66, Ancient Talents slot is opened through a quest.§§Talents placed in the slots can be freely replaced as needed. When specific combinations of talents are placed on§different slots, additional special effects will be activated. However, Ancient§Talents cannot gain additional special abilities through combinations.","8594":"*Equipment Basics§Equipment is able to change a character's appearance and increases their stats.§The equipment types and qualities (color) are listed as follows.§§Equipment Types§1. One-Handed Weapons: sword, axe, hammer.§2. Two-Handed Weapons: greatsword, battle axe, warhammer, staff, firearm, bow, and ammunition.§3. Armor: Head, Chest, Hands, Legs, Feet.§§Equipment Color1. White: Basic equipment.§§2. Green: Strong equipment that offers some stat boosts.§3. Orange: Powerful items that only sprites can craft. They offer good stat bonuses and special effects§when multiple pieces of the same set are worn.§4. Blue: Rare equipment with superior stat bonuses.§5. Yellow: Epic equipment. Properties unknown...§Fashion§§Costume pieces will be displayed in place of normal equipment, if worn, but do not replace your§actual equipped weapons and armor.","8595":"*Enhance:§Basic equipment can be enhanced through fortification.§Every success adds a marker of +1, +2, +3,§up to +18 after the Item name.§§To enhance equipment, find fortification craftsmen in the cities.§Enhancing requires weapon scrolls§for weapons and armor scrolls for armor.§§Fortifying will affect weapon lumination. Repeated fortifying to +5§makes a weapon emit a brilliant white light. Weapons will glow brightest at +18.§§§*Crafting:§Talent cards and resonance stones may be synthesized by alchemists in§the cities, provided they have the necessary talent blueprints or resonance§stone formulas.§§*Inlay:§If a piece of equipment has empty slots, resonance stones can be inlaid in§those slots to add abilities to equipment.§§Inlaying 2 or more of the same type of resonance stone§will add a lighting effect to one-handed weapons, while inlaying 4 resonance stones of the§same type will add this effect to a 2-handed weapon.§§§When attacking with weapons or offensive magic, or for specific targets, all§resonance stones inlaid in your equipment will resonate and increase the power of your attacks.","8596":"*Normal Mission: May receive mission from NPC once requirements are fulfilled.§Unable to receive mission again after completing it.§§*Repeat Mission: May receive mission from NPC once requirements are fulfilled.§May receive mission again after completing it.§§*Book EXP.§Guild Quests are given by the Guild Affairs Officers posted in the large cities.§§*Order Requests: When assigning Sprites tasks,§they will sometimes bring back order scrolls, which can be used to receive material collection quest.§After collecting the materials specified in the order,§the quest can be completed at the designated NPC in each city.§You can repeat these quests as many times as The§lower the displayed value, the faster the speed. The maximum attack speed§is 0.8 seconds per attack.§◎ACC Attribute: Affects whether Normal Attack or skills hit the target.§Normal Attack and physical skills of bows, spears, and cannons are considered§R-ATK ACC. Normal Attack and physical skills of other weapons are considered§melee ACC. All magic skills are considered melee ACC.§◎Against Classes: Additional attack attributes applied to specific classes.§◎True Attribute Physical DMG: When using Normal Attack or physical skills,§deals DMG to the target that ignores Resistance.§◎True Attribute Magic DMG: When using magic skills, deals DMG to the§target that ignores Resistance.§◎Specialization DMG: Additional attack attributes applied to specific monster§types, including equipment and status effects. True Attribute DMG and§Specialization DMG are not affected by CRIT bonuses but are influenced§by DMG increase or reduction effects.§§Defense Attributes§◎DMG Reduction: The final reduction during DMG calculation, including DMG§caused by attributes, specialization, and reflection.§◎PEN Resistance: Reduces the PEN attribute applied by the target, with a§maximum value of 100%.§◎CRIT Resistance: Reduces the chance of the target dealing a Fatal Blow to you.§◎CRIT DMG Resistance: Reduces the damage ratio of a Fatal Blow§dealt by the target.§◎Block Chance: The chance to block Normal Attack and physical skills§when equipped with a shield.§◎Block DMG Reduction: When a block is successful, further reduces DMG by 80%.§◎Damage Reflection: Reflects a portion of the DMG received back to§the attacker, influenced by DMG increase or reduction effects.§◎Against Classes: Additional defensive attributes applied to specific classes.§§Other Attributes§◎Move SPD: The character's movement speed, with a base value of 100% and§a maximum value of 300%.§◎Unstoppable Casting Chance: The chance to avoid being interrupted by attacks§while casting skills.§◎Aggro Value: Affects the level of aggro generated toward monsters.§◎Heal Value: Increases the bonus amount when healing a target.§◎Healing Received: Increases the bonus amount when receiving healing.§Heal Value and Healing Received only affect certain statuses and skills.§◎EXP Bonus: Additional EXP bonus multiplier, with a maximum value of 400%.§◎Item Drop Bonus: Increases the probability of monsters dropping items when killed.§When the Item Drop Bonus reaches the maximum value of 100%, monsters with§item drops will always drop items when killed.§◎Blood-draining Normal Attack: Restores a portion of HP proportional to the DMG§dealt to the target with Normal Attack.§◎Blood-draining Skill: Restores a portion of HP proportional to the DMG dealt to the§target when using skills. Some blood-draining attributes are not affected by§Heal Value or Healing Received effects.§◎Normal Attack MP Draining: Restores a portion of MP proportional to the DMG§dealt to the target with Normal Attack.§◎Skill MP Draining: Restores a portion of MP proportional to the DMG dealt to the§target when using skills. If the target has no MP, even if MP Draining is triggered,§no MP will be restored.","17650":"Melee Phys. CRIT Rate","17651":"Melee Physic Critical Rate","17652":"Ranged Phys. Only one of the options—close, medium, or far—can§be selected. You can also set a \"center point\" so the character returns§to specific coordinates to control the movement range.§＊Auto Avoid - When checked, the character will avoid monsters that§meet the set conditions during automatic movement. The \"Classic\" column displays the experience needed for each level in the classic game mode, while the \"Reincarnated\" column shows the experience required in the reincarnated mode.","level":"Level","levels":"Levels","xp_classic":"Classic XP","xp_reinca":"Reincarnated XP","leveling_guide_title":"Effective Leveling Guide","leveling_guide_content":"\u003Cdiv class=\"alert alert-dark\">\n  \u003Ch4>Preparation before leveling up to 80+\u003C/h4>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>Reach level 66, then immediately reincarnate at level 31 (unless you need reincarnation 100 to get the 5% bonuses).\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Before leveling up to 65+, if necessary, get a potion that offers +800 magic defense points by completing the Oceans Company reputation to 16k. Turn in 1200 \"ancestral ornaments\" in the repeatable quests between levels 63 and 65.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Get power-leveled from level 66 to 80 if possible. Make minimal investment in specializations that offer offensive stats (attack, critical damage, critical rate, penetration) and defensive stats (defense, critical rate reduction, sufficient movement).\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Acquire level 3 or 4 talents that offer significant offensive stats or a very powerful combination of talents. It is unnecessary to complete all talents.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Before leveling up to 80+, if you are not a mech or traveler class, you must get 50k reputation with Kaslow, Ilya, and Techno, as well as 25k of each gathering reputation from the 3 towns (e.g., Miners' Syndicate), 16k with Elsa, and at least 16k at the Frost Altar.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>If you are a mech or traveler class, your level 1 performance stones are available at the altars on different maps.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>If you do not want to get all the level 1 performance stones, you will need at least 16k reputation with Techno to access the +400 physical defense potion.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>For Elsa reputation farming (levels 78 to 80 for repeatable quests), you need at least 16k to access the physical reduction potion (-4% physical damage taken) and 25k for the magic reduction potion (-4% magic damage taken).\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\u003C/div>\n\n\u003Cdiv class=\"alert alert-dark\">\n  \u003Ch4>Leveling up to 80+: Basic Concepts\u003C/h4>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>Mob grinding from level 80 to 90 is generally done on the 4D maps Ancient Polar Realm (north or south of the map) or Moonlight Forest (near the scorpions or cats). Test spots to find where you are most comfortable. Transmuted dungeons (TPT or Laby T) can be an option, but I do not recommend them.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>After reincarnation, levels 86 to 90 are the longest part of XP farming. Each level takes much longer than levels closer to 100 because starting at level 94, you will have access to the Transmuted KEFT dungeon, which gives twice as much XP per mob as the 4D mobs.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>There are quest lines that must be done as they give access to dungeons and important titles to farm. Check the full list in the GF guide for details.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>To level up quickly to 80+, alternate between 4D mob grinding (duo is very profitable) and quest lines on Temporal Rift maps, which give a lot of XP since a recent update. Details on the maps to visit and the method to follow will be provided in the GF guide.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Sometimes, an event called \"Hunting Event\" available from an NPC in Kaslow can cost 3k gold, but the mobs in this level 100 zone give four times more XP than the 4D mobs. Check the patch notes to see if the event is available and refer to the solution in the GF guide for more details.\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\u003C/div>\n\n\u003Cdiv class=\"alert alert-dark\">\n  \u003Ch4>Complete Guide to Leveling Up to 80+: Mob Grinding / Quests to Do\u003C/h4>\n\n  \u003Ch5>Levels 66 to 82:\u003C/h5>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>You have access to the Kaslow in Flame Dungeon quest line starting at level 66 with \"Desperate Call for Help.\" Follow the KEF quest guide until the quest \"Advance on the Front Line 3.\"\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Starting from the quest \"Very Suspicious 1\" available from \"Butler Tomas in the Kaslow Desert Area,\" you will access the \"Rebel Base\" for KEF farming starting at level 86 and Transmuted KEF from level 94.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>To reach level 82, do 4D mob grinding (duo is recommended). You will need enough movement, 9-10k physical defense, more than 150 evasion, and at least 20-25k attack, 100 critical rate, and 300 critical damage.\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\n  \u003Ch5>Levels 82 to 85:\u003C/h5>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>In the Kaslow Desert Area, you will find a passage to the map \"Peninsula of Sorrow.\" Complete the main quests to access the maps \"Glass Mountain\" and \"Plateau of Hope.\"\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Stop at Glass Mountain and switch to 4D mob grinding to reach level 85. You can also do profitable repeatable quests and Daily Quests (DQ) or take advantage of the Hunting Event if available.\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\n  \u003Ch5>Levels 86 to 88:\u003C/h5>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>Resume KEF quests with Tomas in the Kaslow Desert Area and complete the quest line in Rebel Base. You will also have access to the Kaslow in Flame Dungeon to get the \"KEF Tome.\"\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>Continue the main quests in \"Glass Mountain\" and \"Plateau of Hope\" until level 87. At this level, start the quests in the 91+ zones based on your progress.\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\n  \u003Ch5>Levels 88 to 90:\u003C/h5>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>Start the \"Condemned Island\" quests to obtain important titles. You can also buy the titles from the Black Market Vendor to save time.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>From level 88, you can advance in the quests on the \"Plateau of Hope\" and \"Smoking Basin\" maps. Be sure to complete the zone quests up to level 91 to unlock access to the Distorted Dimension dungeon (DD).\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\n  \u003Ch5>Levels 91 to 94:\u003C/h5>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>Complete all quests on the last trio of maps: Smoking Basin, Valley of Light, and Steep Port. Mob grinding is generally not necessary if you follow the quests correctly.\u003C/li>\n  \u003C/ul>\n\n  \u003Ch5>Levels 94 to 100:\u003C/h5>\n  \u003Cul>\n    \u003Cli>From level 94, focus on the KEFT dungeon at Rebel Base. Make sure you have enough defensive stats to manage the rushes and optimize your equipment for efficient grinding.\u003C/li>\n    \u003Cli>From level 96, you can return to Steep Port to unlock quests
